title: Pump Operators, Except Wellhead Pumpers
description: Tend, control, or operate power-driven, stationary, or portable pumps and manifold systems to transfer gases, oil, other liquids, slurries, or powdered materials to and from various vessels and processes.

tasks.text: Monitor gauges and flowmeters and inspect equipment to ensure that tank levels, temperatures, chemical amounts, and pressures are at specified levels, reporting abnormalities as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Record operating data such as products and quantities pumped, stocks used, gauging results, and operating times.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Plan movement of products through lines to processing, storage, and shipping units, using knowledge of interconnections and capacities of pipelines, valve manifolds, pumps, and tankage.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Turn valves and start pumps to start or regulate flows of substances such as gases, liquids, slurries, or powdered materials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Communicate with other workers, using signals, radios, or telephones, to start and stop flows of materials or substances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Connect hoses and pipelines to pumps and vessels prior to material transfer, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Tend vessels that store substances such as gases, liquids, slurries, or powdered materials, checking levels of substances by using calibrated rods or by reading mercury gauges and tank charts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean, lubricate, and repair pumps and vessels, using hand tools and equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Read operating schedules or instructions or receive verbal orders to determine amounts to be pumped.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test materials and solutions, using testing equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Tend auxiliary equipment such as water treatment and refrigeration units, and heat exchangers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Add chemicals and solutions to tanks to ensure that specifications are met.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collect and deliver sample solutions for laboratory analysis.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pump two or more materials into one tank to blend mixtures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
